title: Investigate and extract support card event skills
description: Investigate `single_mode_story_data` + reward/conclusion tables to find how support card events grant skills (e.g., Kitasan SSR event → Professor of Curvature). Map story events → skill rewards per support card. Populate `eventSkills` array. If DB path not found, document limitation and design extensible mechanism.
notes: Investigated single_mode_story_data, event conclusion/choice reward, and reward set tables. Current Global master.mdb exposes support card stories but no reliable story->skill reward relation; extractor preserves eventSkills in merge mode and emits empty arrays in replace mode for future extensibility.

title: Extract support cards collection (support-cards.json)
description: New extractor `extract-support-cards.ts` that produces `support-cards.json`. Each document: `{ id, name, charaId, charaName, rarity, supportCardType, hintSkills: [...], eventSkills: [...] }`. Base data from `support_card_data`, names from `text_data` category 76/77.
notes: Implemented extract-support-cards.ts and generated src/modules/data/json/support-cards.json with base support card fields.

title: Denormalized data extraction pipeline
description: Normalize master.mdb extraction to produce self-contained JSON documents for browser consumption. Each JSON = one collection, each entry = fully resolved document with no cross-collection joins at runtime. Covers skills, support cards, and umas.
acceptance_criteria:
- skills.json documents embed family, gene versions, uma source display info, support card display info
- New support-cards.json collection with hint skills and event skills
- umas.json documents embed outfit details, unique skills, support cards
- Services simplified to lookups over richer documents
- No runtime getByGroupId or cross-collection joins
- Bundle size delta checked and acceptable

title: Enrich umas.json with outfit details and skills
description: Each outfit includes `{ id, epithet, skills: [...] }` with learnable skills from `available_skill_set` (with needRank), unique skill reference, and associated support cards via `chara_id`.

title: Extract support card hint skills
description: From `single_mode_hint_gain` where `hint_gain_type=0`, join via `support_card_data.skill_set_id = hint_id`. Populate `hintSkills` array on each support card document with skill id, name, rarity.
notes: Populated hintSkills from single_mode_hint_gain hint_gain_type=0 joined via support_card_data.skill_set_id=hint_id and skill_data/text_data.

title: Embed uma source display info in skill sources
description: Each entry in `sources` should include resolved `name`, `outfit` from text_data/umas instead of just outfitId. Eliminates runtime lookups in resolveUmaSource.
close_reason: Implemented in 70c08b3: extractor embeds uma source display name and outfit.

title: Embed gene version bidirectional refs in skills.json
description: If a skill has gene_version, embed `{ id, name, rarity }` of the inherited version. If it's an inherited skill, embed reference to the original unique. Both directions pre-resolved.
close_reason: Implemented in 70c08b3: extractor embeds bidirectional unique/gene references.

title: Embed skill family in skills.json
description: Each skill document should include `family: Array<{ id, name, rarity, iconId }>` pre-resolved from groupId siblings. Eliminates runtime `getByGroupId` calls.
close_reason: Implemented in 70c08b3: extractor embeds pre-resolved skill family references.

title: Bundle size check for denormalized JSONs
description: Verify that denormalized skills.json, support-cards.json, and enriched umas.json don't inflate the bundle excessively. Check gzipped sizes. If too large, consider embedding only IDs + name instead of full objects for cross-references.

title: Simplify runtime services for denormalized data
description: Simplify SkillService (remove getByGroupId runtime query, families pre-embedded), UmaService (outfit details pre-embedded), and add SupportCardService. Update skills.tsx to use embedded display info instead of runtime resolvers.
